mainEntity.name: What kind of compensation can I get for permanent hearing damage? Will they pay for hearing aids?

acceptedAnswer.text: You are entitled to 100% medical coverage, including hearing aid replacement for life, and a disability settlement if your loss meets the state's impairment threshold. A lawyer helps ensure you receive top-tier medical equipment.
acceptedAnswer.description: Utah and Idaho workers' compensation should cover 100% of your medical evaluations, the cost of high-quality hearing aids, and periodic replacement of those devices (usually every 3-5 years). Additionally, if your hearing loss meets a certain threshold of "functional impairment," you may be entitled to a lump-sum cash settlement for Permanent Partial Disability. If the tinnitus (ringing) is so severe it affects your ability to sleep or concentrate, that can sometimes be factored into the overall disability rating. You should never pay a deductible or co-pay for these work-related medical needs. An attorney ensures you get the "premium" hearing aids, not just the cheapest version the insurance wants to buy.
